mu (based on "kamu" but toss away that "ka" and use "mu")

aku mencintaimu = I love u

1. cinta - often used to express how u feel about sm1 and telling them that u love them.
2. sayang - often used not only for expressing ur feelings to ur loved one but u can use it to give some support, heals, and help for other people (this words can be used for both gender. if u a boy, u can use it to ur friend too to support him, and same for girl)

10/25/25, 17:49 - ‎Skorpiooon: The Lovers represent relationships and choices. Its appearance in a spread indicates some decision about an existing relationship, a temptation of the heart, or a choice of potential partners. Often an aspect of the querent's life will have to be sacrificed; a bachelor(ette)'s lifestyle may be sacrificed and a relationship gained (or vice versa), or one potential partner may be chosen while another is turned down. Whatever the choice, it should not be made lightly, as the ramifications will be lasting.
10/25/25, 17:50 - ‎Skorpiooon: High Priestess is a card of mystery, stillness and passivity. This card suggests that it is time to retreat and reflect upon the situation and trust your inner instincts to guide you through it. Things around you are not what they appear to be right now.

10/28/25, 16:05 - ‎Skorpiooon: Ikan Tongkol Balado (Spicy Chili Tuna)
A classic Indonesian dish where firm mackerel tuna is fried and then mixed with a rich, spicy sambal (chili) sauce.
The sauce is typically made from a blended paste of chili, shallots, and garlic, often with added aromatics like ginger or lime leaves.
Often served with steamed rice as a staple meal.

11/5/25, 16:17 - ‎Skorpiooon: Joseph E. LeDoux checked brain activity after high-stress events (exams, breakups, deadlines, even surgeries)

His discovery was actually quite unexpected.

He found that emotional pain didn’t peak during the stress.
It peaked after.

When the mind decides you’re safe, the body begins to release all the cortisol it’s been storing to keep you functional.

That release (the shaking, crying, zoning out, or emotional exhaustion) is your nervous system FINALLY actually processing.

Our bodies just delay it because they think we’re too busy dealing with the danger.

Basically, your body doesn’t have the luxury to feel when survival mode is on.

So it waits.

Until you hand in the project, leave the hospital, or walk away from the person who hurt you.

Then suddenly, you fall apart.
But it’s not falling apart… it’s falling back into yourself.

Cortisol drops. Serotonin rebalances. The parasympathetic system reactivates.

Sørensen called it the emotional aftershock.
A delayed response designed to keep you alive first, and let you feel second.

So if you’re crying when things are calm, it means you’re finally safe enough to heal ❤️‍🩹

You want to learn about psychology?

Then @coolgirlpsych is the page for you.

Join our community of 21000+ cool ppl and get your daily learnings 💌

Source: LeDoux, J. E., & Brown, R. (2017). A higher-order theory of emotional consciousness. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS), 114(10), E2016–E2025.*
https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1619316114
